dialogue.line: Ah! Ah! That's what I'm talkin' about.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Really?

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I was thinkin' that same thing, man.

jokes.text: Retard. I'm talking about taking Dennis to Atlantic City... for a couple of days until the election is over.
jokes.setup: Charlie planning elaborate murder
jokes.punchline: Mac revealing he just meant a vacation
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection payoff, shows character differences perfectly

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Retard. I'm talking about taking Dennis to Atlantic City... for a couple of days until the election is over.

jokes.text: If we used piano wire on him for a sec... As long as we hurt him a little bit...
jokes.setup: Charlie accepting the Atlantic City plan
jokes.punchline: Charlie still trying to incorporate violence into the harmless plan
jokes.explanation: Good Charlie persistence with his violent ideas

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: If we used piano wire on him for a sec... As long as we hurt him a little bit...

jokes.text: Apparently, you called him a nerd, Mac. - Some people don't take kindly to that type of insult.
jokes.setup: Police explaining how Mac got caught
jokes.punchline: The 'nerd' insult leading to Mac's downfall
jokes.explanation: Excellent long-term callback showing consequences

dialogue.speaker: Police Officer
dialogue.line: Apparently, you called him a nerd, Mac. - Some people don't take kindly to that type of insult.

jokes.text: They're gonna tear through you like Kleenex.
jokes.setup: Police threatening Mac with prison
jokes.punchline: The specific and crude comparison to Kleenex
jokes.explanation: Dark humor typical of the show, good specific imagery

dialogue.speaker: Police Officer
dialogue.line: They're gonna tear through you like Kleenex.

jokes.text: Are you soliciting a bribe from me? - Sorry, pal. That's politics.
jokes.setup: Mac asking if police are soliciting a bribe
jokes.punchline: Police using Mac's own phrase to justify corruption
jokes.explanation: Perfect episode-ending callback, excellent irony and structure

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Are you soliciting a bribe from me?

dialogue.speaker: Police Officer
dialogue.line: Sorry, pal. That's politics.

jokes.text: Are you soliciting a bribe from me? Sorry, pal. That's politics.
jokes.setup: FBI agents seemingly soliciting a bribe from Mac
jokes.punchline: Using Mac's own political justification phrase against him
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback ending - the corrupting phrase comes full circle as system corrupts back

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Are you soliciting a bribe from me?
